methodology_rules_relation: Relationship between data, rules and scientific reference
methodology_intro: The calculation engine relies on accounting rules to distinguish zakatable from non-zakatable items and classify assets.
methodology_classifications_intro: The application classifies financial items into main accounting categories to determine the Zakat base.
methodology_page_intro: This decision support system for corporate Zakat calculation is based on a conceptual accounting framework and peer-reviewed academic research at doctoral level.
methodology_relation_para1: The calculation engine in this application uses accounting rules to distinguish zakatable from non-zakatable items and to classify assets and liabilities according to the conceptual framework proposed in the thesis. Financial data is entered by the user, while the rule engine derived from the academic research applies the accounting standards for Zakat to arrive at the Zakat base and proposed Zakat amount.
methodology_relation_para2: This application is designed as a decision support system to help accountants and auditors document the methodology and review the adopted rules, without replacing professional judgment or specialized Islamic legal advice.
methodology_show_rules_button: Show rules adopted in the calculation
methodology_classification_logic_para: When entering financial items into the system, each item is linked to an accounting category (asset / liability) and a detailed type (e.g. cash, trading goods, production inventory, receivables, fixed assets, short-term liabilities, ...). The rule engine uses this classification to determine whether the item:
methodology_classification_logic_list1: is fully included in the Zakat base,
methodology_classification_logic_list2: is excluded from the Zakat base,
methodology_classification_logic_list3: or requires special treatment (e.g. certain types of debt and investments).
methodology_classification_logic_para2: The rules actually applied in each calculation are documented in the rules section of the calculation detail, where the user can review rule codes, their descriptions, and the reason for applying each rule to a given item.
methodology_cash_desc: E.g. bank balances, cash on hand, current deposits.
methodology_cash_zakat: Fully included in the Zakat base.
methodology_trading_desc: Goods and merchandise held for sale (trading stock).
methodology_trading_zakat: Included in the Zakat base at realizable value (trading goods).
methodology_production_inv_desc: Raw materials, work in progress, and finished goods inventory.
methodology_production_inv_zakat: Classified per the conceptual framework; may require professional and Sharia assessment.
methodology_receivable_desc: Amounts owed to the company by others.
methodology_receivable_zakat: Included in the Zakat base if collectible, with special treatment for doubtful debts.
methodology_fixed_assets_desc: E.g. property, machinery, and software held for use.
methodology_fixed_assets_zakat: Generally not part of the Zakat base; income from them is considered under Sharia rules.
methodology_investment_desc: Shares or holdings intended for long-term retention.
methodology_investment_zakat: May be subject to Zakat depending on the nature of the investment and distribution policy; professional and Sharia assessment may be needed.
methodology_short_liab_desc: Obligations due within one operating cycle or one year.
methodology_short_liab_zakat: Deducted from the Zakat base when due at the time of the Hawl, per the thesis criteria.
methodology_long_liab_desc: Obligations extending beyond one financial year.
methodology_long_liab_zakat: Only the portion due in the Zakat period is deducted, per the adopted accounting methodology.
methodology_equity_capital_desc: Owners’ interest in the company’s net assets.
methodology_equity_capital_zakat: Not treated directly as part of the Zakat base; Zakat is calculated on the net zakatable assets base per the conceptual framework.
equity_note: Equity does not enter the Zakat base; for accounting balance only.
history_intro: View all Zakat calculations for the active company (drafts and finalized)
pdf_generation_failed: Failed to generate PDF. Please try again.
rule_INTENTION_OVERRIDE_FIXED_ASSET: Intention override – fixed asset for trade
rule_CASH_reason: Cash and cash equivalents are fully included in the Zakat base because they are immediately available wealth.
rule_TRADING_GOODS_reason: Goods held for sale are treated as trading inventory and are included in the Zakat base at their realizable value.
rule_PRODUCTION_INVENTORY_reason: Production inventory is classified according to the conceptual framework and may require professional and Sharia assessment before inclusion.
rule_RECEIVABLE_reason: Receivables that are likely to be collected are included in the Zakat base; doubtful debts are treated more cautiously.
rule_FIXED_ASSET_reason: Fixed assets used in production or operations are not themselves zakatable; only their resulting income is considered.
rule_INTANGIBLE_ASSET_reason: Intangible assets such as software or licenses are not directly included in the Zakat base; only the income derived from them may be zakatable.
rule_LONG_TERM_INVESTMENT_reason: Long-term investments are assessed based on their nature and purpose; they are not automatically treated as trading inventory.
rule_SHORT_TERM_LIABILITY_reason: Short-term liabilities that are actually due at the Zakat date are deducted from the Zakat base.
rule_LONG_TERM_LIABILITY_reason: Only the portion of long-term liabilities that is due within the Zakat period is deductible from the Zakat base.
rule_PAID_IN_CAPITAL_reason: Paid-in capital represents owners’ equity and is not itself the Zakat base; Zakat is calculated on the net zakatable assets.
rule_RETAINED_EARNINGS_reason: Retained earnings are part of owners’ equity; Zakat is calculated on the net zakatable assets instead of this balance alone.
rule_RECEIVABLE_STRONG_reason: Strong receivables that are expected to be collected are included fully in the Zakat base.
rule_RECEIVABLE_WEAK_reason: Weak or doubtful receivables are treated cautiously and may be excluded or partially included depending on collection probability.
rule_MIXED_ASSETS_reason: Mixed assets require professional and Sharia judgement to separate zakatable trading elements from non‑zakatable components.
rule_INTENTION_OVERRIDE_FIXED_ASSET_reas: When a fixed asset is actually held for trade rather than use, it is reclassified as trading inventory and becomes zakatable.
rule_INTENTION_OVERRIDE_INVESTMENT_reaso: When a long‑term investment is held with trading intent, it is treated as trading stock and included in the Zakat base.
